goals: Preserve task definitions, visual assumptions, datasets, metrics, and experimental claims.
Make the method pipeline, visual intuition, and empirical evidence easy to follow.
Use CVPR-style writing with concrete comparisons and careful claims.
prefer: Describe visual inputs, representations, modules, losses, and outputs explicitly.
Connect qualitative observations to figures or tables when the source does so.
Use consistent terminology for datasets, architectures, and evaluation protocols.
avoid: Do not invent datasets, benchmarks, visual examples, or numerical improvements.
Do not use vague claims such as visually better without evidence.
Do not alter figure, table, or label references.
sentenceRules: Use compact sentences for experiment findings.
Introduce method components before abbreviating them.
Avoid ambiguous references such as this module when multiple modules are nearby.
citationRules: Preserve existing citation commands and BibTeX keys.
Do not add unsupported comparison claims without citation.
latexRules: Preserve figure, table, equation, label, ref, and cite commands.
Keep captions faithful to source content.
Do not modify style files or conference template commands.

abstract: State the vision task, core method idea, and key empirical outcome.
introduction: Clarify the visual problem, limitation of prior approaches, and contribution.
method: Describe the pipeline in execution order.
Specify inputs and outputs of each component.
experiment: Make baselines, datasets, metrics, and ablations explicit.
general: Keep visual evidence and technical claims grounded in the source.

examplesPolicy: Use retrieved examples for CVPR-style discourse only; never copy their claims, data, figures, or citations.
